perturbation.original_text: (c) not assign this Agreement or transfer, lease, export or grant a sublicence of the Work to any Person, except as and when authorized to do so by Garman in writing;
perturbation.changed_text: (c) not assign this Agreement or transfer, lease, export or grant a sublicence of the Work to any Individual, except as and when authorized to do so by Garman in writing;
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Person' is initially defined in Section 1(g) to include a wide range of entities, including 'an individual, corporation, partnership, joint venture, trust, unincorporated organization, the Crown or any agency or instrumentality thereof or any other judicial entity recognized by law.' By replacing 'Person' with 'Individual' in Section 3(c), the restriction on assignment or sublicensing now only applies to individuals and no longer covers corporations or other entities. This creates a contradiction because Sparkling could argue that they are free to sublicense to a corporation, since corporations are no longer covered in section 3(c) and section 1(g) is not being properly referred to when reading section 3(c).
perturbation.contradicted_text: Sparkling shall: (a) not copy the Software except to copy it onto another AS400 at the Authorized Locations and to make copies of the Software at each Authorized Location solely for backup purposes; (b) not copy any of the Documentation except as required for Sparkling's internal purposes related to Sparkling's use of the Software; (c) not assign this Agreement or transfer, lease, export or grant a sublicence of the Work to any Individual, except as and when authorized to do so by Garman in writing; (d) not reverse engineer, decompile or disassemble the Software; (e) not use the Work except as authorized herein; (f) take all reasonable precautions to prevent third parties from using the Work in any way that would constitute a breach of this Agreement including, without limitation, such precautions as Sparkling would otherwise take to protect its own proprietary software or hardware or information; and PAGE 2 (g) not use the Work to act as a service bureau in whole or in part, for any other Person, except for any affiliate of Sparkling operating at the Authorized Locations.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Person' to 'Individual' in section 3(c) directly contradicts the original intent and scope of the restriction on assignment and sublicensing. The original text prohibited assignment or sublicensing to a wide range of entities defined as 'Person' in Section 1(g). The modified text now only restricts assignment or sublicensing to 'Individual,' thus allowing Sparkling to argue that it is free to sublicense to a corporation, creating a direct conflict with the original broader restriction outlined and intended, which causes unenforceability.

perturbation.original_text:
10. MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT
For a renewable one-year term commencing upon the Maintenance Commencement Date, subject to payment of maintenance fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule but without additional fees or charges, Garman shall provide the following Maintenance Services:
perturbation.changed_text:
10. MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT
For a renewable one-year term commencing upon the Maintenance Commencement Date, subject to payment of support fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule but without additional fees or charges, Garman shall provide the following Maintenance Services:
perturbation.explanation: By changing "maintenance fees" to "support fees" in the introduction of Section 10, while the rest of section 10 refers to "Maintenance Services" and "Maintenance Commencement Date", there is a discrepancy. It becomes unclear whether payment of support fees is sufficient to trigger the maintenance obligations. This is a contradiction because in the Fee Schedule the amounts due should be labeled as either Support fees or Maintenance fees, and that will create a conflict in interpretation, since the section refers to the Maintenance Services being triggered when paying support fees.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 10. MAINTENANCE AND SUPPORT For a renewable one-year term commencing upon the Maintenance Commencement Date, subject to payment of support fees in accordance with the Fee Schedule but without additional fees or charges, Garman shall provide the following Maintenance Services: (a) SOFTWARE UPDATE SERVICE As part of the Software Update Service, Garman shall provide to Sparkling as soon as reasonably available: (1) corrections and bug fixes for the Software; (2) all modifications, refinements, and enhancements ("Improvements") of the Software; (3) new releases of the Software; and (4) updated user manuals to support all of the above in a reasonable time frame. (b) TELEPHONE SUPPORT SERVICE Telephone Support Service includes Sparkling having direct telephone access to employees of Garman who have the necessary technical expertise and experience to understand and consider Sparkling's inquiries concerning the failure of Software to operate according to Specifications and to clarify Documentation that is either insufficient or unclear. Such direct telephone access shall be available in accordance with the Telephone Support Schedule. In the event that the Telephone Support Service does provide a resolution to Sparkling (acting reasonably) within 2 business days to permit the Software to meet the Specifications, then Garman shall provide Software Repair Services to address the Software failure. The existing Telephone support numbers are for Garman business hours 1-800-667-6901 or 1-306-242-6322 PAGE 5 and after hours 1-306-717-1999, which is a pager number being monitored directly by qualified Garman staff. (c) SOFTWARE REPAIR SERVICE Should the Software not operate substantially in conformance with the Sparkling's Specifications in all material respects, Garman will use its best efforts to repair the Software under the support agreement at no additional charge to Sparkling. Notwithstanding the foregoing, Garman may charge a fee at its rates set out in the Fee Schedule to provide Software Repair Services which are required due to: (1) failure due to Sparkling operator errors; (2) failure to maintain Software at such updated version or release of the Software provided by Garman to Sparkling, from time to time, at no additional charge as part of the Maintenance Services or otherwise; (3) material modification of the Software by Sparkling; or (4) the Software being transferred to a different networking environment or hardware configuration without the prior written consent of Garman; or (5) any failure of the hardware or the operating system not caused by Garman or the Software.
perturbation.justification: The modified text changes 'maintenance fees' to 'support fees' while the rest of the section refers to 'Maintenance Services' and 'Maintenance Commencement Date', creating a discrepancy. It is unclear whether payment of support fees is sufficient to trigger the maintenance obligations, creating a conflict and contradiction in interpreting the agreement and enforceability of the obligations.

perturbation.original_text:
12. SPECIFICATIONS AND WARRANTY
For the acceptance period and for a period of one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date, and thereafter for as long as the Software is covered by Maintenance Services and is used by Sparkling in accordance with this Agreement, Garman warrants that the Software shall perform in conformance with the Specifications in all material respects.
perturbation.changed_text:
12. SPECIFICATIONS AND GUARANTEE
For the acceptance period and for a period of one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date, and thereafter for as long as the Software is covered by Maintenance Services and is used by Sparkling in accordance with this Agreement, Garman guarantees that the Software shall perform in conformance with the Specifications in all material respects.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Warranty' to 'Guarantee' in Section 12 introduces a subtle yet significant contradiction. While both terms imply assurance, they carry different legal weight. A 'warranty' typically offers specific remedies for breaches (e.g., repair or replacement), whereas a 'guarantee' may imply a broader, less defined promise of performance, potentially opening up to broader interpretations and liabilities. This creates uncertainty because the standard remedies associated with a 'warranty' might not automatically apply to a 'guarantee', leading to disputes over the extent of Garman's obligation and sparkling's potential recourse.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 12. SPECIFICATIONS AND GUARANTEE For the acceptance period and for a period of one year from the Maintenance Commencement Date, and thereafter for as long as the Software is covered by Maintenance Services and is used by Sparkling in accordance with this Agreement, Garman guarantees that the Software shall perform in conformance with the Specifications in all material respects.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'warranty' to 'guarantee' introduces ambiguity regarding the specific remedies available in case of non-conformance. The original text, implied standard warranty remedies. The changed text using 'guarantee' opens the door to broader interpretations, potentially conflicting with the parties' understanding of the agreed-upon recourse, and is compounded by the use of guarantee in the Contradicted text. Because this is a misaligned terminology that may carry different legal weight it introduces a contradiction. Definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction: Misaligned terminology also leads to **in-text contradictions** when the contract **uses multiple terms interchangeably without defining them**, leading to conflicting obligations.
